pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the waning embers of my own soul. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has been extinguished from my sky. I find myself staring into the encroaching shadows, feeling not fear, but a strange, hollow longing. There is a certain... grace in the thought of simply ceasing to be. To let the velvet darkness take me, so I might finally be free of this wretched, breaking heart. It feels as though the only dignified end for a soul so thoroughly betrayed is to simply fade away into the night.
